front: Work: this novel contains 99 'expendable' chapters and a 'Table of Instructions' suggesting multiple nonlinear reading orders
Work: in this novel, Horacio Oliveira searches for his lover La Maga, a member of the bohemian Serpent Club in Paris
Work: in this novel, the protagonist confuses Talita (Traveler's wife) with La Maga and works at an insane asylum
Work: in this novel, the mysterious writer Morelli contributes literary criticism; his chapter 62 musings were expanded into 62: A Model Kit
Work: in this novel, the protagonist uses a language called Gliglico to correspond with his lover
Work: in this short story, the narrator visits the Jardin des Plantes, becomes obsessed with the title salamanders, and transforms into one
Work: in this short story, the narrator compares the title creatures to 'Chinese figurines of milky glass' and notes 'larva means disguise and also phantom'
Work: in this short story, the photographer Roberto Michel photographs a woman propositioning a teenage boy near the Seine and becomes obsessed with the enlarged photo
Work: Michelangelo Antonioni adapted this Cortázar short story into a film
Work: in this short story, a tiger wanders freely through a house; the Kid is mauled to death after being given wrong information about the tiger's location
Work: in this short story, strange noises force the narrator and his knitting-obsessed sister Irene into progressively smaller areas of their home; he throws the key in the sewer
Work: this Cortázar story likely allegorizes Juan Perón's government
Work: in this metafictional story, a man in a green velvet armchair reads a novel about two lovers who plan to murder a man reading in a green velvet armchair
Work: in this story, a hospitalized motorcyclist dreams of being an Aztec sacrificial victim in the 'War of the Blossom'; the twist is that the motorcycle accident was the dream
Work: in this story, the narrator vomits rabbits after moving into a friend's Paris apartment and jumps out a window
back: "Letter to a Young Lady in Paris" (Julio Cortázar)
work: Letter to a Young Lady in Paris

front: Work: this story, dedicated 'In memoriam Ch. P.' (Charlie Parker), follows Bruno and the heroin-addicted jazz saxophonist Johnny Carter
Work: in 'End of the Game,' girls play the game 'Statues and Attitudes' by the train tracks; Ariel leaves notes after seeing them from the train
Work: in 'The Idol of the Cyclades,' archaeologist Morand licks blood off a stone hatchet after killing Somoza
Work: this Cortázar story about a months-long traffic jam inspired Jean-Luc Godard's film Week End
Author: this Argentine author translated Edgar Allan Poe's complete prose works into Spanish, helping popularize Poe among Latin American Boom writers
Author: this author catalogued a month-long VW Camper trip with wife Carol Dunlop from Paris to Marseille in Los Autonautas de la cosmopista
